UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a job seeker's profile, including personal details, skills, and links to subscription information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication and contact.

bio.description: A short biographical summary provided by the user.

linkedinProfileUrl.description: URL to the user's LinkedIn profile.

currentJobTitle.description: The user's current or most recent job title.

currentCompany.description: The company where the user is currently or most recently employed.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

stripeCustomerId.description: The customer ID generated by Stripe for this user, used for payment and subscription management.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

JobPosting.title: JobPosting
JobPosting.description: Represents a job listing extracted from an external source like LinkedIn.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobPosting entity.

title.description: The title of the job position.

companyName.description: The name of the company offering the job.

location.description: The geographical location of the job.

jobUrl.description: The original URL of the job posting (e.g., LinkedIn job link).

description.description: The full description of the job.

postedDate.description: The date when the job was originally posted.

salaryRange.description: The stated salary range for the position (e.g., '$50k - $70k').

skillsRequired.description: A list of skills required for the job.

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the job details were extracted and saved.

scrapedByUserId.description: Reference to UserProfile. Indicates which user initiated the scraping of this job. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N JobPosting)

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Tracks a user's interaction and progress with a specific job posting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

status.description: The current status of the application (e.g., 'Saved', 'Applied', 'Interview', 'Rejected', 'Offered').

applicationDate.description: The date when the user officially applied for the job.

notes.description: Personal notes or reminders about this application.

cvDocumentId.description: Reference to CVDocument. The specific CV document used for this application. (Relationship: CVDocument 1:N Application)

coverLetterDocumentId.description: Reference to GeneratedDocument. The specific cover letter document used for this application. (Relationship: GeneratedDocument 1:N Application)

matchScore.description: The AI-generated match score indicating fit for this job (0-100).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this application record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this application record was last updated.

CVDocument.title: CVDocument
CVDocument.description: Stores details about a user's uploaded CV document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CVDocument entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. The user who owns this CV. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CVDocument)

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded CV.

fileUrl.description: URL to the stored CV file (e.g., PDF in cloud storage).

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the CV document was uploaded.

isPrimary.description: Indicates if this is the user's primary or default CV.

GeneratedDocument.title: GeneratedDocument
GeneratedDocument.description: Stores documents (CVs, cover letters) generated by the AI assistant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedDocument entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. The user for whom the document was generated. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GeneratedDocument)

jobPostingId.description: Reference to JobPosting. The specific job posting for which this document was generated. (Relationship: JobPosting 1:N GeneratedDocument)

content.description: The full text content of the generated document.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was generated.

promptUsed.description: The input or prompt text used to generate this document with AI.

fileUrl.description: Optional URL to the stored generated document file (e.g., PDF).

matchScore.description: The AI-generated match score associated with this document, if applicable (0-100).

Plan.title: Plan
Plan.description: Defines the different subscription tiers available in the application (e.g., Free, Pro).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Plan entity.

name.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., 'Free', 'Pro').

description.description: A description of the features and benefits included in the plan.

price.description: The price of the plan in the specified currency.

currency.description: The currency in which the plan is priced (e.g., 'USD', 'ETB').

interval.description: The billing interval for the plan (e.g., 'month', 'year', 'lifetime').

aiGenerationsPerDayLimit.description: The maximum number of AI generations allowed per day for this plan. Null indicates unlimited.

maxSavedJobs.description: The maximum number of job postings a user can save under this plan. Null indicates unlimited.

stripeProductId.description: The corresponding product ID in Stripe for this plan.

features.description: A list of features included in this plan.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Manages a user's active or past subscription to a plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. The user who holds this subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Subscription)

planId.description: Reference to Plan. The plan this subscription is for. (Relationship: Plan 1:N Subscription)

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'Active', 'Cancelled', 'Trialing', 'PastDue', 'Ended').

startDate.description: The date when the subscription started.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is scheduled to end or has ended (e.g., due to cancellation or expiration).

stripeSubscriptionId.description: The corresponding subscription ID in Stripe for this subscription.

currentPeriodStart.description: The start of the current billing period.

currentPeriodEnd.description: The end of the current billing period.

cancelledAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription was cancelled, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

AIGenerationLog.title: AIGenerationLog
AIGenerationLog.description: Records each instance of an AI generation event by a user, used for tracking usage limits.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIGenerationLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. The user who performed the AI generation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AIGenerationLog)

generatedDocumentId.description: Reference to GeneratedDocument. The specific document generated by this AI action. (Relationship: GeneratedDocument 1:1 AIGenerationLog)

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI generation occurred.

type.description: The type of AI generation performed (e.g., 'CV', 'CoverLetter', 'MatchScore', 'InterviewQuestions').

definition.description: Stores private user profile information, including sensitive details like 'stripeCustomerId'. Access is restricted to the owner matching 'userId' in the path, or administrators. The 'isAdmin' field is implicitly handled by the '/appRoles/admin/{adminId}' collection for DBAC compliance.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: A global, publicly readable collection of job postings aggregated by the platform. Write access is restricted to authenticated users (who scrape the jobs) or administrators. No authorization dependencies on parent data.

params.name: jobPostingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific job posting.

definition.description: Private collection storing a user's specific application tracking records. Ownership is strictly determined by the 'userId' in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this application.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific application record.

definition.description: Private collection for a user's uploaded CV documents. Ownership is determined by the 'userId' in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this CV document.

params.name: cvDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific CV document.

definition.description: Private collection for AI-generated documents (e.g., tailored CVs, cover letters) specific to a user. Ownership is determined by the 'userId' in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user for whom the document was generated.

params.name: generatedDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific generated document.

definition.description: A global, publicly readable collection defining the different subscription tiers (Free, Pro). Accessible by all users to view available plans. No authorization dependencies on parent data.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific subscription plan.

definition.description: Private collection tracking a user's active or past subscriptions. Ownership is determined by the 'userId' in the path. Includes denormalized 'aiGenerationsPerDayLimit' and 'maxSavedJobs' from the associated 'Plan' for Authorization Independence, avoiding `get()` calls to the /plans collection in rules for limit checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds this subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific subscription record.

definition.description: Private collection logging each AI generation event for a user. Ownership is determined by the 'userId' in the path. Used for server-side enforcement of usage limits.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the AI generation.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific AI generation log entry.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) to define administrators. The mere existence of a document at this path indicates that the 'adminId' (which corresponds to a 'userId') has administrative privileges. Documents can be empty.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for EthioHire is designed with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This is achieved primarily through hierarchical path-based ownership for user-specific data and selective denormalization for metadata that might otherwise require `get()` calls in security rules. All user-specific data, such as `Applications`, `CVDocuments`, `GeneratedDocuments`, `Subscriptions`, and `AIGenerationLogs`, are nested under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`. This structure ensures that the `userId` is directly available in the path (`request.auth.uid`), eliminating the need for `get()` operations to check document ownership. For instance, a user can only read/write documents in `/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)/applications/{applicationId}`, making rules simple (`allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`).

Additionally, to prevent `get()` calls for subscription limits when evaluating access for AI generations or saved jobs, key plan attributes (`aiGenerationsPerDayLimit`, `maxSavedJobs`) are denormalized directly into the user's `Subscription` document (within `/userProfiles/{userId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}`). While the actual enforcement of daily AI generation limits will primarily be handled by a Cloud Function (as Firestore rules are not ideal for aggregate counts across documents), this denormalization makes any potential rule evaluation more efficient and atomic by providing necessary data directly within the accessed document or its immediate parent, adhering to the principle of not relying on 'parent' data for authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports efficient queries with predictable access patterns (QAPs) by: 
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures is stored in separate collections. Global, publicly readable data like `JobPostings` and `Plans` are in top-level collections (`/jobPostings/{jobPostingId}`, `/plans/{planId}`). These collections can be efficiently queried (`list`) without complex filters in security rules, as all documents within them share the same broad access rights (e.g., read-all).
2.  **Path-Based Ownership for Private Data:** For user-owned data, nesting under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...` means that clients can efficiently query (list) all documents for *their own* `userId` without rules needing to filter on a `userId` field. The query itself implicitly includes the `userId` in the path, which is then validated by `request.auth.uid` in the rules.
3.  **DBAC via Existence:** Administrative privileges are managed via a dedicated `appRoles/admin/{adminId}` collection, allowing a simple and efficient `exists()` check in rules for determining admin status without exposing sensitive role information on the `UserProfile` document itself.

**Other Principles Adherence:**
*   **Structural Segregation:** `JobPostings` are global, `Plans` are global, and all user-specific data is strictly segregated under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`. This prevents mixing data with different access requirements in the same collection. 
*   **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used extensively for user-owned data. Global roles are managed by existence in a dedicated collection. 
*   **Data Clarity and Predictability:** Semantic naming conventions are used for all paths and wildcards (e.g., `{userId}`, `{jobPostingId}`). State fields (like `Application.status`, `Subscription.status`) are explicit.

This design minimizes rule complexity, prevents security vulnerabilities arising from `get()` dependencies, and ensures efficient query performance for common application workflows.
